response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong full-stack engineering fundamentals and is currently working on MCP and agentic infrastructure at re:cinq, the resume lacks documented evidence of shipping production-grade AI solutions over the required 8+ years. The pivot to AI-agent work is promising but too recent (1.5 months) to satisfy the core Staff-level requirement of leading AI prototype-to-production transitions. Critical technical gaps include no Python experience, no vector database or RAG architecture background, and limited multi-cloud exposure.
strengths: Strong TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production environments
Currently contributing to MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, directly aligned with job responsibilities
Proven data infrastructure experience from Dunnhumby role with high-performance systems and real-time data pipelines
concerns: No demonstrated experience building and shipping production AI/ML solutions—the core requirement for a Staff-level AI engineer
Critical technology gaps: Python not listed despite being a required language, no vector database or RAG experience mentioned
AI-agent work is recent (started April 2025) and insufficient to meet 8-year AI development requirement; limited evidence this extends beyond current infrastructure work

key_factors.factor: Missing Core Technologies (Python, Vector DBs, RAG)
